title: Ardisia albipedicellata
intro: Ardisia albipedicellata, a species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae, is native to tropical regions and is commonly known as the 'Tropical Coralberry'. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ardisia albipedicellata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Ardisia albipedicellata is a species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. It is native to China and belongs to the genus Ardisia.
